generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance AdaptiveCruiseControlActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "ROS2-based microcontroller handling adaptive cruise control logic with real-time feedback loops for dynamic speed adjustments in highway driving scenarios"
]

instance SteeringActuator : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Steering servo component for autonomous vehicle control, integrated with ROS2 middleware for real-time synchronization with digital twin"
]

instance MotorActuator : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Motor control unit for vehicle propulsion, interfaced with ROS2 for adaptive cruise control implementation"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ActuationCommands
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance HokuyoLiDAR : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR sensor providing high-resolution 3D point clouds for spatial sensing in autonomous vehicle environment"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance IntelDepthCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Intel Deepsense D435i Depth Camera generating depth maps for environmental perception and obstacle detection in autonomous vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:producedData DepthMapData
]

instance VehicleSpeedSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Embedded sensor measuring vehicle velocity in m/s for adaptive cruise control feedback"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateData
]

instance EnvironmentalMonitor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Combination of embedded sensors monitoring environmental conditions (e.g., temperature, lighting) for adaptive driving logic"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateData
]

// C4: Data Transmission Components
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoLiDAR
]

instance DepthMapData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelDepthCamera
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleSpeedSensor, EnvironmentalMonitor
]

instance ActuationCommands : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringActuator, MotorActuator
]

// C5: Data Processing Components
instance PointCloudProcessingFE : DTDFVocab:FunctionalEntity [
    base:desc "Software component performing point cloud clustering and tracking to filter noise and extract meaningful object tracks from LiDAR data"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EnvironmentalPerceptionModel
]

instance DataPreProcessingFE : DTDFVocab:FunctionalEntity [
    base:desc "Data preprocessing module aligning vehicle state data with ROS2 message formats and unit conversions (e.g., m/s to km/h)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AdaptiveCruiseControlModel, VehicleStateModel
]

instance DataTranslationFE : DTDFVocab:FunctionalEntity [
    base:desc "Component ensuring message type alignment between physical sensors and digital twin entities via ISO 23247 standards"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AdaptiveCruiseControlActuator
]

// C6: Models/Data
instance AdaptiveCruiseControlModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "ROS2-based control algorithm model implementing adaptive cruise control logic with real-time feedback loops for dynamic speed adjustments"
]

instance EnvironmentalPerceptionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "3D spatial model derived from LiDAR depth camera data for obstacle detection and environmental mapping"
]

instance VehicleStateModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamic vehicle state representation tracking position, velocity, and acceleration in real-time"
]

instance PointCloudProcessingData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudProcessingFE
]

instance DepthMapData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    DTDFVocab:fromData DepthMapData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EnvironmentalPerceptionModel
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AdaptiveCruiseControlModel, VehicleStateModel
]

instance ActuationCommands : DTDFVocab:Data [
    DTDFVocab:fromData ActuationCommands
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AdaptiveCruiseControlActuator
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ROS2Middleware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2-based middleware facilitating interoperability between physical sensors, digital models, and edge devices for real-time data exchange"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDataMonitoring, ComputationOffloading
]

instance NVIDIAJetson : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Edge computing platform with AI acceleration capabilities (lightweight neural networks) for processing high-compute tasks in autonomous vehicle twin"
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloading
]

instance MATLABSimulink : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulation environment for modeling control algorithms (indirectly referenced for adaptive cruise control validation)"
]

instance ISO23247Standards : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Digital Twin Framework for Manufacturing (ISO 23247-1) and concepts (ISO/IEC 30173) providing foundational guidelines for entity-based architecture"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataTranslationFE
]

instance PythonCPlusPlus : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Programming languages used for algorithm development and data processing pipelines in autonomous vehicle digital twin"
]

// C12: Services
instance RealTimeDataMonitoring : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time streaming and visualization of sensor data (vehicle speed, acceleration, obstacle detection) for engineers, test pilots, and AI model developers"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeVisualizationInsight, HistoricalTrendAnalysis
]

instance ComputationOffloading : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Offloads high-compute tasks (object detection, path planning) to edge devices for low-latency processing in autonomous vehicle control"
    DTDFVocab:provides EdgeProcessingAction
]

instance RemoteManagement : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides teleoperation and remote control capabilities for emergency interventions and debugging of physical twin systems"
    DTDFVocab:provides EmergencyTeleoperationAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Live visualization of sensor data streams (LiDAR point clouds, depth maps, vehicle state metrics) for immediate situational awareness"
]

instance HistoricalTrendAnalysis : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Historical performance benchmarking of autonomous vehicle systems through data trend analysis over time"
]

instance EdgeProcessingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic offloading of AI processing tasks to edge devices (NVIDIA Jetson) for real-time autonomous vehicle control"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EmergencyTeleoperationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Manual remote control interface for emergency intervention in autonomous vehicle systems"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C13: Environment
instance AutonomousVehicleEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains HokuyoLiDAR, IntelDepthCamera, VehicleSpeedSensor, EnvironmentalMonitor,
              AdaptiveCruiseControlActuator, SteeringActuator, MotorActuator,
              PointCloudProcessingFE, DataPreProcessingFE, DataTranslationFE,
              ROS2Middleware, NVIDIAJetson, MATLABSimulink
]

instance HighwayDrivingScenario : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Operational domain characterized by dynamic traffic conditions and highway driving scenarios with safety-critical decision-making requirements"
]

// C14: Component Relationships
instance AutonomousVehicleSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "1/10th-scale autonomous vehicle with adaptive cruise control system serving as physical twin under study"
    base:contains AutonomousVehicleEnvironment
]

instance AdaptiveCruiseControlSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "ROS2-based control system implementing adaptive cruise control logic with real-time feedback loops"
    base:contains AdaptiveCruiseControlModel, AdaptiveCruiseControlActuator
]

instance PerceptionSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Environmental perception system using LiDAR and depth cameras for obstacle detection"
    base:contains HokuyoLiDAR, IntelDepthCamera, EnvironmentalPerceptionModel,
            PointCloudProcessingFE, DataPreProcessingFE
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
Not in Document
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system enforces **time synchronization** between physical and digital components with a **100 ms accuracy requirement**, derived from hardware limitations and safety-critical vehicular applications. This ensures precise coordination for computation offloading, where devices must share a unified time reference to maintain event ordering, data integrity, and reliable coordination across the system. The implementation does not mandate sub-100 ms latency; instead, it enforces a maximum of 100 ms for critical operations, aligning with standards that mandate stricter synchronization for vehicular applications."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "
  The Digital Twin system follows an **entity-based architecture** rooted in **ISO 23247 Reference Architecture (RA)**, partitioned into three primary entities:
  1. **Physical Twin Entity**: Hosts compute, sensor, and actuator resources, enabling general automotive functionality (e.g., *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking*). Deployed on hardware-specific components, this entity diverges from ISO 23247’s *Object Management Entity (OME)* by encapsulating system-wide resources.
  2. **Digital Twin Entity**: Manages data preprocessing, actuation, and control mechanisms, distinct from ISO 23247’s centralized FE mapping. This entity consolidates cross-entity data workflows.
  3. **User Entity**: Organized into *User Interface FE*, split into *3D Visualization* and *Control Panel* components, reflecting user-centric interaction layers.

  **Technologies**: The system implements **ROS2 (Robot Operating System 2)** as the middleware, with each Functional Entity (FE) instantiated as an independent ROS2 package. Each package contains one or more ROS2 nodes, ensuring modularity and interoperability. The architecture emphasizes **entity-based ownership** of functionality, impacting engineering workflows by decoupling resource-specific and cross-cutting logic.
  "
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The ISO 23247 standard emphasizes **real-time and high-fidelity** Digital Twin systems, where the **Digital Twin Entity** must mirror the Observable Manufacturing Element (OME) with synchronized clocks to ensure temporal accuracy. While the standard does not provide explicit validation/verification (V&V) methodologies, the **Data Collection and Device Control Entity** ensures synchronized data streams to mitigate uncertainty. The absence of detailed uncertainty quantification or error detection mechanisms is noted, though the system’s reliance on standardized protocols (e.g., OPC UA) implies inherent fidelity through interoperability. No specific fidelity thresholds, model calibration methods, or quality assurance processes are described in the provided context."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin technical connection** relies on a hybrid network architecture combining **ROS2-based intra-system communication** and **Wi-Fi UDP inter-entity communication** for low-latency data exchange. Key specifications include:

**Communication Protocols**:
- **ROS2 (Robot Operating System 2)** handles intra-system communication between software components (e.g., Actuation FE, Data Translation FE) via ROS messages, ensuring modularity and interoperability.
- **Wi-Fi UDP** enables inter-entity communication between the digital twin and physical vehicle, with a strict **<100ms latency requirement** (ISO 23247 R7) to support real-time control loops.

**Network Topology & Architecture**:
- The system architecture (Fig. 2) shows a **centralized deployment model** where sensor data (LiDAR, motor, steering) is processed locally on the **NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano** microcomputer onboard the vehicle, with external data storage handled via Wi-Fi.
- **No explicit redundancy mechanisms** are documented, but the reliance on ROS2 and UDP suggests resilience to minor network disruptions (e.g., packet loss handled by ROS2’s reliability features).

**Bandwidth & Latency Requirements**:
- The system prioritizes **real-time responsiveness**, with latency constraints explicitly stated as **≤100ms** for critical data streams (e.g., vehicle state, actuation commands).
- Bandwidth allocation is implied for high-frequency data (e.g., LiDAR updates) but not quantified in the documents.

**Security Protocols**:
- **No explicit mention of TLS or encryption** for ROS2/Wi-Fi communication in the provided context. Security is assumed to be handled by the underlying OS (e.g., Linux-based ROS2 deployment) or vehicle’s embedded security stack.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin hosting and deployment** infrastructure is optimized for **on-vehicle edge computing** with minimal reliance on centralized cloud resources. Key specifications include:

**Hosting Platforms**:
- **Primary Hosting**: The **Physical Twin Entity** runs on the **NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano** microcomputer onboard the vehicle, processing real-time sensor data (LiDAR, motor, steering) via software components like **Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking** and **Actuation FE**.
- **Secondary Hosting**: External data storage (e.g., ego-vehicle state logs) is mandated to reside on a device **external to the vehicle**, likely via cloud/edge servers for long-term analysis.

**Computational & Storage Requirements**:
- **Computational Load**: The Jetson Orin Nano handles high-frequency data streams (e.g., LiDAR point clouds, actuation commands) with minimal latency, leveraging ROS2 for efficient inter-process communication.
- **Storage**: Raw data (e.g., point clouds, vehicle telemetry) is stored externally to comply with system requirements, avoiding onboard storage bottlenecks.

**Scalability Mechanisms**:
- **No explicit scalability details** are provided for the onboard system, but ROS2’s modular design supports horizontal scaling if additional onboard compute resources are added.
- **Deployment Models**: The architecture supports **real-time deployment** for control loops (e.g., ACC) and **batch processing** for data storage/reporting via **Reporting FE** and **Data Collecting FE**.

**Availability & Reliability Assurance**:
- **Onboard Reliability**: The Jetson Orin Nano is assumed to provide **high availability** for real-time tasks, with no redundancy mechanisms explicitly documented.
- **External Storage**: The system mandates **external storage** to ensure data persistence beyond vehicle operation, but no SLAs or redundancy guarantees are specified for this component.
"
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "
  Not in Document
  "
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "This Digital Twin system adheres to the following **standards and specifications**:
  - **Core Standards**:
    - **ISO 23247** (Parts 2 and 4): Defines the **entity-based reference architecture** (e.g., Observable Manufacturing Elements, Data Collection and Device Control Entity, Digital Twin Entity, User Entity) and **information exchange protocols**.
    - **OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA)**: Standardized communication interface for data exchange between Functional Entities (FEs) and OMEs.
  - **Technologies and Protocols**:
    - **ROS2 (Robot Operating System 2)**: Used for implementing Functional Entities (FEs) as modular packages, enabling interoperability and scalability.
    - **ISO 15622**: Likely referenced for data modeling and representation standards in manufacturing.
    - **3GPP (3rd Generation Partnership Project)**: Mentioned in the broader context of automotive systems, possibly for vehicle-to-everything (V2X) communication.
  - **Certification and Compliance**:
    - **McSCert (McLaren Supplier Certification)**: The provided GitHub repository and demonstration link suggest compliance with McSCert’s automotive supply chain standards, though the documents do not detail certification verification processes explicitly.

  **Note**: The documents explicitly mention ISO 23247, OPC UA, ROS2, ISO 15622, and 3GPP, but do not provide details on certification frameworks or compliance frameworks beyond McSCert’s mention in the context of automotive DTs."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system must adhere to the safety-critical requirements outlined in ISO 26262-1:2018 for functional safety in road vehicles, ensuring compliance with safety integrity levels (ASIL) and fail-safe mechanisms. Additionally, the system must implement Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) functionalities as specified in ISO 15622:2018, which mandates performance requirements and test procedures for ACC systems to ensure reliable and safe autonomous driving operations. While no explicit mention of cybersecurity protocols or threat mitigation strategies is provided in the documents, the system must inherently respect the safety-critical nature of the underlying vehicle systems, emphasizing risk assessment approaches aligned with functional safety standards."
]
